residue_numbering: 1-indexed positions in the 266-aa ProteinGym target sequence
source: A4GRB6 itself is inactive/deleted in UniProt, so the label uses VIM-2 reference records Q5U7L7 and Q9K2N0 plus conserved class B metallo-beta-lactamase motif mapping. See data/proteingym/source_records/uniprot_vim2_metal_site_reference.json.
evidence: The ProteinGym sequence is length 266 and differs from reviewed VIM-2 Q5U7L7 at one non-site position, I185 versus V185. Q5U7L7 is a reviewed VIM-2 record with Zn-binding evidence at positions 114, 116, and 198 from PDB 6BM9. Q9K2N0 is an unreviewed Pseudomonas aeruginosa VIM-2 record with PDB-backed Zn-binding features at 114, 116, 118, 179, 198, and 240 across VIM-2 structures including 1KO2, 1KO3, 4BZ3, 4NQ2, 5ACU, 5ACW, and 5ACX. The six-residue label is therefore a conservative metal-site/mechanism-critical slice for evaluation only.
